headline: How Shopify chooses between RDS, Aurora, DynamoDB, and ElastiCache for 2 million stores
why: Shopify powers 2 million online stores — from a one-person shop to Kylie Cosmetics. Each store has different data: product catalogs, orders, sessions, analytics. One database type cannot serve all needs efficiently.

topics.usage: Shopify stores orders in RDS PostgreSQL — not flat files. When a merchant filters orders by date + status + customer, the DB executes one SQL query in milliseconds. A file scan would take minutes.

Merchant data, product catalogs, orders, and inventory all live in RDS Aurora (MySQL-compatible). Structured, relational — products have foreign keys to variants, variants to inventory, inventory to warehouses.

Shopify's cart and session data lives in DynamoDB — millions of concurrent shopping carts, each a JSON document. Schema-free: one cart has 2 items, another has 200 with custom attributes. DynamoDB handles both at millisecond latency.

Product page data is cached in ElastiCache Redis. When 50,000 people view the same product during a flash sale, 49,999 requests hit Redis (microseconds) — only 1 in 1,000 goes to RDS. DB stays alive during traffic spikes.

diagram:
graph TD
    User([🛒 Shopper]) --> CF[CloudFront CDN]
    CF --> Cache[ElastiCache Redis\nProduct pages\nMicrosecond reads]
    Cache -->|Cache miss| RDS[RDS Aurora\nProducts, Orders\nInventory]
    User --> Cart[Shopping Cart\nDynamoDB\nMillisecond, schema-free]
    Merchant([🏪 Merchant]) --> RDS
    RDS --> Analytics[Redshift\nSales analytics\nHistorical data]
    style User fill:#1e293b,color:#94a3b8
    style Merchant fill:#1e293b,color:#94a3b8
    style Cache fill:#7c3aed,color:#fff
    style Cart fill:#1e3a5f,color:#dbeafe
    style RDS fill:#1a3320,color:#86efac
    style Analytics fill:#450a0a,color:#fca5a5
takeaway: Shopify doesn't use one database — they use the right database for each data type. Relational for structured merchant data, NoSQL for flexible cart data, in-memory for performance. This is the real-world answer to 'when do you use what.'
